meaning: n.重新分配,立法机构中席次之重新分配
examples: The big winner from reapportionment is Texas, which will gain four seats.
As a result, the 2010 reapportionment gives Texas four additional House seats.
There's a similar lesson in the fact that Florida gains two seats in the reapportionment and New York loses two.
The 2010 Census will also kick off the dramatic political spectacle that is reapportionment.
Again, the Census determines -- the Census based on obvious population shifts determines reapportionment and representation in Congress.
